The **2009–10 Segunda División** season (known as the [Liga Adelante](/wiki/Liga_Adelante "Liga Adelante") for sponsorship reasons) was the 79th since its establishment. The first matches of the season were played on 29 August 2009, and the season ended on 19 June 2010. [Real Betis](/wiki/Real_Betis "Real Betis"), [Numancia](/wiki/CD_Numancia "CD Numancia") and [Recreativo de Huelva](/wiki/Recreativo_de_Huelva "Recreativo de Huelva") are the teams which were relegated from [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga") the previous season. The teams which were promoted from [Segunda División B](/wiki/Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n_B "Segunda División B") were: [Cádiz](/wiki/C%C3%A1diz_CF "Cádiz CF") (champion), [Cartagena](/wiki/FC_Cartagena "FC Cartagena") (runner-up), [Real Unión](/wiki/Real_Uni%C3%B3n "Real Unión") (play-off winner) and [Villarreal B](/wiki/Villarreal_CF_B "Villarreal CF B") (play-off winner).

The first goal of the season was scored by [Quique de Lucas](/wiki/Enrique_de_Lucas "Enrique de Lucas"), who scored a seventh-minute goal for Cartagena against [Girona](/wiki/Girona_FC "Girona FC") in the early kick-off. The first red card of the season was given to [David González](/wiki/Francisco_David_Gonz%C3%A1lez_Borges "Francisco David González Borges") from [Las Palmas](/wiki/UD_Las_Palmas "UD Las Palmas") in their opening game against [Real Sociedad](/wiki/Real_Sociedad "Real Sociedad"). The first [hat-trick](/wiki/Hat-trick "Hat-trick") was scored by [Cristhian Stuani](/wiki/Cristhian_Stuani "Cristhian Stuani") in the match between [Castellón](/wiki/CD_Castell%C3%B3n "CD Castellón") and [Albacete](/wiki/Albacete_Balompi%C3%A9 "Albacete Balompié").
